$comment: Machine-readable requirement inventory; the generated compliance-report.md is its human-readable view. tools/compliance-gate.ts fails the build if any id here has zero passing tests, or if a test references an id-shaped tag absent from this list.

namespaces.LW: Lacewing-native requirements (the README's common-mistakes list and the library's own guarantees)

requirements.summary: Verifier uses only caller-specified algorithms; the token header never chooses.
Each key is bound to exactly one algorithm at import time.
The alg header must match the algorithm the key/signature actually uses.
Only cryptographically current algorithms live in the registry.
Never produce or consume the 'none' algorithm; it is unrepresentable.
RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 and other weak algorithms are absent from the registry.
Cryptographic agility: the algorithm registry is the single point of change.
Validate all cryptographic operations; reject the token if any fails.
Validate cryptographic inputs (curve points, signature encoding) - no psychic signatures.
Keys must have sufficient entropy; minimum length enforced at import.
Human-memorizable passwords are rejected as HMAC keys.
No compression: the zip header is unsupported and rejected.
Headers and claims are strict UTF-8; malformed or non-canonical encodings reject.
Keys are validated as belonging to the issuer; the key source is scoped to the profile/issuer.
The sub claim is validated when the profile pins one.
aud must be present and validated; absent or mismatched audience rejects.
Untrusted kid is sanitized (length cap + charset) before any lookup.
No SSRF via jku/x5u: URL-bearing headers are never followed.
Issued tokens carry an explicit typ (constructor argument on sign).
The verifier checks the expected typ; the profile requires one.
Validation rules for different JWT kinds are mutually exclusive (profiles + property test).
No decode-without-verify on the verify path; unsafeDecode output is branded UntrustedJwt, incompatible with VerifiedJwt (compile-time proof).
Sign rejects claim names matching the sensitive-name heuristic.
Sign rejects claim values matching high-confidence secret patterns (Luhn cards, PEM blocks, nested JWTs).
Documentation states JWS payloads are readable plaintext.
Every signed token carries a unique jti by default.
Profiles accept a RevocationStore; a revoked jti causes rejection.
The store is consulted only after signature and claims pass (ordering assertion).
Store errors fail closed unless explicitly overridden.
Sign rejects lifetimes beyond the profile cap.
Verify enforces maxTokenAge independently of exp.
The cookie helper always emits HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite - weaker configs are unrepresentable.
The bearer parser is strict RFC 6750: no query-string tokens, single token only.
Documentation warns against localStorage/sessionStorage and shows the cookie/memory alternatives.
Cookbook access/refresh profiles are mutually exclusive by typ (property test over them).
Docs/examples/generators default to asymmetric (EdDSA); HMAC docs lead with the symmetric-distribution caveat.
Encrypted-JWT (JWE) algorithms are curated: none, RSA1_5, RSA-OAEP-SHA1 and PBES2* are unrepresentable, and a dir key must match its content-encryption size.
Decryption uses only the profile's allowlisted key-management (alg) and content-encryption (enc) algorithms; the token header never decides.
Encrypted tokens carry the same claim discipline (typ/iss/aud/exp, unique jti, lifetime cap), enforced on encrypt and validated on decrypt.
JWS and JWE never cross over: a JWS handed to jwtDecrypt and a JWE handed to jwtVerify are both rejected.
